title: DAVID — Old Norse: Runes Attested, Sagas Reconstructed

gate_0.music_plan: original score only — music cleared, cue sheet on file

brand.title: DAVID · The Archive
brand.subtitle: Dead languages, actually pronounced.
brand.cta: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.

beats.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, Old Norse — the language of sagas and runestones, where carved stone gives us attested forms and manuscript poetry demands reconstruction.
Vikings spread their speech across the North Atlantic. When the age of settlement ended, the tongue evolved — but runes and skaldic verse froze pieces of its sound for us.
Where does the corpus speak with its own mouth — and where do we supply the breath?

The Jelling stones, the Rök runestone, and younger futhark inscriptions attest spellings and names. The Poetic Edda in later Icelandic copies preserves meter that constrains how words must have sounded.
We read runic graphs against Old West Norse phonology, use alliterative patterns in fornyrðislag, and cross-check Snorri's prose — separating inscription evidence from normalized saga prose.
A runestone line is attested as carved. Full saga delivery with normalized vowels is reconstructed — both labels appear on screen when they matter.
Listen — Haraldr konungr sigrsæll — King Harald, blessed with victory — as attested on the Jelling stone, beside a reconstructed skaldic line from the meter.
beats.on_screen: ATTESTED · Jelling runes · RECONSTRUCTED · skaldic meter

beats.speech_text: Join the Archive — and hear the North again, carved in stone and counted in verse.
